info.title: CluedIn REST API — Entities
info.description: Read, create, modify, merge, split, and inspect golden records and their metadata.

get.description: Returns an entity straight from the blob data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. When `organizationId` is supplied the request impersonates that organisation. Admin-only.
Returns an entity straight from the blob data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. When `organizationId` is supplied the request impersonates that organisation. Admin-only.

Returns the encrypted blob backing an entity as a downloadable file, decrypting in place and setting the original filename, MIME type, MD5 (if known), and modification date on the response. Admin-only.

Returns the encrypted blob backing an entity as a downloadable file, decrypting in place and setting the original filename, MIME type, MD5 (if known), and modification date on the response. Admin-only.
Returns the processed-entity record from the blob data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. Returns 404 if the processed entity does not exist. Admin-only.
Returns the processed-entity record from the blob data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. Returns 404 if the processed entity does not exist. Admin-only.
Returns the processed entity record as a downloadable, compressed file with `.processedentity` filename suffix. Admin-only.
Returns the processed entity record as a downloadable, compressed file with `.processedentity` filename suffix. Admin-only.
Returns an entity straight from the graph data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. Admin-only.
Returns an entity straight from the graph data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. Admin-only.
Returns an entity straight from the search data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. Admin-only.
Returns an entity straight from the search data store, serialised with the persisting flavor. Admin-only.
Returns the profile of every organisation in the platform. Admin-only.
Returns the profile of every organisation in the platform. Admin-only.
Returns entity-code lookup results matching the supplied code, scoped to the impersonated organisation. Admin-only.
Returns entity-code lookup results matching the supplied code, scoped to the impersonated organisation. Admin-only.
Returns every provider registered in the system (category, details, id, enabled flag, name, type), ordered by name. Admin-only.
Returns every provider registered in the system (category, details, id, enabled flag, name, type), ordered by name. Admin-only.

get.summary: Admin: list entity types by type

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Entity type technical name to filter by.

get.description: Returns every entity type known to the platform that matches the supplied type filter. Admin-only.

get.summary: Admin: list entity types by type

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Entity type technical name to filter by.

get.description: Returns every entity type known to the platform that matches the supplied type filter. Admin-only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: True to enable, false to disable.

post.description: Sets a provider's `IsEnabled` flag and publishes a `ProviderEnabledNotification` or `ProviderDisabledNotification`. Returns 404 if the provider id cannot be found. Admin-only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: True to enable, false to disable.

post.description: Sets a provider's `IsEnabled` flag and publishes a `ProviderEnabledNotification` or `ProviderDisabledNotification`. Returns 404 if the provider id cannot be found. Admin-only.

get.description: Returns a dictionary of every key/value pair from the application configuration (`AppSettings`). Admin-only.
Returns a dictionary of every key/value pair from the application configuration (`AppSettings`). Admin-only.

Returns the agent-job records stored for the organisation. The endpoint accepts optional `organizationId`, `from`, and `to` parameters (the date range parameters are accepted but currently ignored — the same underlying query is used either way). Admin-only.

Returns the agent-job records stored for the organisation. The endpoint accepts optional `organizationId`, `from`, and `to` parameters (the date range parameters are accepted but currently ignored — the same underlying query is used either way). Admin-only.
Returns the webhook definitions stored for the organisation (or the impersonated organisation when `organizationId` is supplied). Admin-only.
Returns the webhook definitions stored for the organisation (or the impersonated organisation when `organizationId` is supplied). Admin-only.

Stub endpoint currently routed to the agent-jobs query under impersonated organisation context. The name suggests it is intended for impersonation-token generation; treat the current response shape as implementation-defined. Admin-only.

Stub endpoint currently routed to the agent-jobs query under impersonated organisation context. The name suggests it is intended for impersonation-token generation; treat the current response shape as implementation-defined. Admin-only.

delete.description: Dispatches a `DeleteEntityCommand` for the supplied entity id under impersonated organisation. Returns 200 once the command has been queued. Admin-only.
Dispatches a `DeleteEntityCommand` for the supplied entity id under impersonated organisation. Returns 200 once the command has been queued. Admin-only.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Prefix to match against vocabulary key names.

get.description: Returns vocabulary keys across every vocabulary in the organisation whose name starts with `q`. Admin-only.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Prefix to match against vocabulary key names.

get.description: Returns vocabulary keys across every vocabulary in the organisation whose name starts with `q`. Admin-only.

post.summary: Admin: build a preview image part

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Either a URL to download or a byte array (depending on overload).

post.description: Builds a `RawDataPart` of type `/RawData/PreviewImage` from a supplied URL (downloads it server-side) or a raw byte array, and returns the resulting image reference. Admin-only.

post.summary: Admin: build a preview image part

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Either a URL to download or a byte array (depending on overload).

post.description: Builds a `RawDataPart` of type `/RawData/PreviewImage` from a supplied URL (downloads it server-side) or a raw byte array, and returns the resulting image reference. Admin-only.

get.summary: Admin: get vocabulary keys by type
get.description: Returns vocabulary keys across every vocabulary in the organisation whose name equals `type`. Admin-only.

get.summary: Admin: get vocabulary keys by type
get.description: Returns vocabulary keys across every vocabulary in the organisation whose name equals `type`. Admin-only.
Reads an entity from the blob store and synthesises a `Clue` that mirrors its codes, properties, edges, and metadata, then serialises the clue. Useful for re-ingesting an entity. Admin-only.
Reads an entity from the blob store and synthesises a `Clue` that mirrors its codes, properties, edges, and metadata, then serialises the clue. Useful for re-ingesting an entity. Admin-only.

post.description: Forces a merge of the supplied source entity ids into a target entity (under impersonated organisation), running the merge pipeline with `ForceMerging=true` and `ManualMergeActionKind`. Admin-only.
Forces a merge of the supplied source entity ids into a target entity (under impersonated organisation), running the merge pipeline with `ForceMerging=true` and `ManualMergeActionKind`. Admin-only.
Removes duplicate relationships on the supplied entity in the graph data store. Admin-only.
Removes duplicate relationships on the supplied entity in the graph data store. Admin-only.

get.description: Returns the duplicate-entity queries registered on the server (the strategies used to find potential duplicates), with their technical name and display name.
Returns the duplicate-entity queries registered on the server (the strategies used to find potential duplicates), with their technical name and display name.
Alias for `GET /api/v1/duplicates/queries` exposed under the historical singular `/duplicate` path. Same response shape and semantics.

parameters.name: queryNames
parameters.description: Optional list of query names. Supports comma, semicolon, or pipe as separators.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Optional entity type to restrict the search to.

get.description: Runs the registered duplicate-entity queries (optionally restricted to a comma/semicolon/pipe-separated list of query names) and returns the groupings of potential duplicates each query produced. Returns 404 if any requested query name is not registered.

parameters.name: queryNames
parameters.description: Optional list of query names. Supports comma, semicolon, or pipe as separators.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Optional entity type to restrict the search to.

get.description: Runs the registered duplicate-entity queries (optionally restricted to a comma/semicolon/pipe-separated list of query names) and returns the groupings of potential duplicates each query produced. Returns 404 if any requested query name is not registered.

get.summary: List potential duplicate entity groupings (alt path)

parameters.name: queryNames
parameters.description: Optional list of query names. Supports comma, semicolon, or pipe as separators.

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Optional entity type to restrict the search to.

get.description: Alias for `GET /api/v1/duplicates/potential-duplicate-entity-groupings`. Same response shape and semantics.

parameters.name: resultSetKey
parameters.description: Result set key inside the query result.

parameters.name: itemGroupingKey
parameters.description: Grouping key inside the result set.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size when using page-based paging. Defaults to 20.

get.description: Returns paged entity instances inside a single grouping returned by a duplicate-entity query. Supports cursor- or page-based paging. Returns 404 if the query name is not registered.

parameters.name: resultSetKey
parameters.description: Result set key inside the query result.

parameters.name: itemGroupingKey
parameters.description: Grouping key inside the result set.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size when using page-based paging. Defaults to 20.

get.description: Returns paged entity instances inside a single grouping returned by a duplicate-entity query. Supports cursor- or page-based paging. Returns 404 if the query name is not registered.

get.summary: List potential duplicate entity instances (alt path)

parameters.name: resultSetKey
parameters.description: Result set key inside the query result.

parameters.name: itemGroupingKey
parameters.description: Grouping key inside the result set.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size when using page-based paging. Defaults to 20.

get.description: Alias for `GET /api/v1/duplicates/potential-entity-duplicate-instances`. Same response shape and semantics.

parameters.name: vocabularyKeys
parameters.description: List of vocabulary keys to match on.

parameters.name: ruleType
parameters.description: Rule type used to combine the key matches.

get.description: Returns potential duplicates for an entity type using a dynamic vocabulary-key query — typically a set of keys and a rule type chosen by the caller. Used when the duplicate detection strategy is built on the fly rather than registered as a named query.

parameters.name: vocabularyKeys
parameters.description: List of vocabulary keys to match on.

parameters.name: ruleType
parameters.description: Rule type used to combine the key matches.

get.description: Returns potential duplicates for an entity type using a dynamic vocabulary-key query — typically a set of keys and a rule type chosen by the caller. Used when the duplicate detection strategy is built on the fly rather than registered as a named query.

parameters.name: vocabularyKeys
parameters.description: List of vocabulary keys to match on.

parameters.name: ruleType
parameters.description: Rule type used to combine the key matches.

get.description: Alias for `GET /api/v1/duplicates/potential-dynamic-duplicates`. Same response shape and semantics.

post.description: Returns the entity instances that match a dynamic key mapping for a given entity type. Pass the key mapping in the body. Returns 400 with validation details when the model is invalid.
Returns the entity instances that match a dynamic key mapping for a given entity type. Pass the key mapping in the body. Returns 400 with validation details when the model is invalid.

post.summary: Get potential dynamic duplicate entities (alt path)
post.description: Alias for `POST /api/v1/duplicates/potential-dynamic-duplicates-entities`. Same request and response shape.

get.description: Returns the entity with the supplied id, augmented with editable-field permissions, suggested searches, and per-provider account data so the UI can render the entity card. The query supports a `full` flag that switches to the raw blob-store serialisation. For Mail entities the body is read from the search store when missing from the primary store, and HTML detection wraps plain text in `<pre>` tags. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs, 403 when the caller lacks access to any of the entity's providers, and 404 when the entity does not exist.

Returns the entity with the supplied id, augmented with editable-field permissions, suggested searches, and per-provider account data so the UI can render the entity card. The query supports a `full` flag that switches to the raw blob-store serialisation. For Mail entities the body is read from the search store when missing from the primary store, and HTML detection wraps plain text in `<pre>` tags. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs, 403 when the caller lacks access to any of the entity's providers, and 404 when the entity does not exist.

Returns the user records (organisation provider accounts) that map to the providers contributing data to the entity. Useful to show who can act on an entity through which provider. Returns 404 if the entity does not exist.

Returns the user records (organisation provider accounts) that map to the providers contributing data to the entity. Useful to show who can act on an entity through which provider. Returns 404 if the entity does not exist.

parameters.name: disableGrouping
parameters.description: When true, return every edge individually instead of grouping by edge type.

get.description: Returns the incoming and outgoing edges for an entity, with edge grouping applied when the number of edges of the same type exceeds the configured `Entity.EdgeGroupingThreshold` (default 25). Set `disableGrouping=true` to receive every individual edge. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs and 404 if the entity does not exist.

parameters.name: disableGrouping
parameters.description: When true, return every edge individually instead of grouping by edge type.

get.description: Returns the incoming and outgoing edges for an entity, with edge grouping applied when the number of edges of the same type exceeds the configured `Entity.EdgeGroupingThreshold` (default 25). Set `disableGrouping=true` to receive every individual edge. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs and 404 if the entity does not exist.

get.summary: Get edges centered on an entity

parameters.name: maxNumberOfEdges
parameters.description: Hard cap on returned edges. Defaults to 500.

parameters.name: includeIncomingOnCenter
parameters.description: Include incoming edges on the centre entity. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: includeIncomingOnOther
parameters.description: Include incoming edges on neighbouring entities. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: includeShadowEntities
parameters.description: Include shadow entities. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: includeTemporalEntities
parameters.description: Include temporal entities. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: includeExternalData
parameters.description: Include externally-sourced entities. Defaults to false.

get.description: Returns the entities and edges reachable within `depth` hops of the supplied entity. Use the toggles to control whether incoming edges, shadow entities, temporal entities, and external data participate. Returns 404 if the centre entity does not exist. The response includes an `IsLimitedByNumberOfRelationships` flag indicating whether `maxNumberOfEdges` was reached.

parameters.name: edgePropertiesHash
parameters.description: Hash identifying the specific edge instance.

get.description: Returns the properties stored on a specific edge between two entities. Returns 404 if either endpoint cannot be found.

post.description: Publishes a service-bus command marking the entity as sensitive. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs and 200 once the command has been published. Same handler as the PUT variant.

put.description: Same handler as `POST /api/entity/sensitive`; both verbs are accepted for backwards compatibility.

post.description: Returns the supplied value with the anonymisation utility applied for the caller's organisation. Returns 400 for empty input. Same handler as the PUT variant.

put.description: Same handler as `POST /api/property/anonymise`; both verbs are accepted.

post.description: Returns the supplied value with anonymised tokens replaced by the original values. Returns 400 when the value is empty or does not contain `<anonymised>...</anonymised>` markers. Same handler as the PUT variant.

put.description: Same handler as `POST /api/property/deanonymise`; both verbs are accepted.
Same handler as `POST /api/entity/unsensitive`; both verbs are accepted for backwards compatibility. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.description: Publishes a service-bus command clearing the sensitive flag on the entity. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs and 200 once the command has been published. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim. Same handler as the PUT variant.

get.description: Returns the entity property schema known to the platform. With the `key` query parameter, the response is filtered to schema entries whose key or display name contains the value, and includes additional metadata such as editability, removability, and obsolete state.

Returns the entity property schema known to the platform. With the `key` query parameter, the response is filtered to schema entries whose key or display name contains the value, and includes additional metadata such as editability, removability, and obsolete state.

Returns an entity by its code. Two query shapes are supported: a single `id` parameter that contains a serialised entity code, or `type` + `origin` + `value` as separate components. Returns 400 for invalid codes, 404 if no entity matches, and 403 when the caller lacks access. With the three-part form the response is just the entity id rather than the full entity card.

Returns an entity by its code. Two query shapes are supported: a single `id` parameter that contains a serialised entity code, or `type` + `origin` + `value` as separate components. Returns 400 for invalid codes, 404 if no entity matches, and 403 when the caller lacks access. With the three-part form the response is just the entity id rather than the full entity card.
Returns the Person entity whose CluedIn email entity code matches the supplied address. Returns 400 for malformed addresses, 404 if no Person matches, and 403 when the caller lacks access.
Returns the Person entity whose CluedIn email entity code matches the supplied address. Returns 400 for malformed addresses, 404 if no Person matches, and 403 when the caller lacks access.

get.summary: Get organization entity by email domain
get.description: Returns the Organization entity whose CluedIn email-domain entity code matches the supplied domain. Returns 400 for empty input, 404 if no Organization matches, and 403 when the caller lacks access.

get.summary: Get organization entity by email domain
get.description: Returns the Organization entity whose CluedIn email-domain entity code matches the supplied domain. Returns 400 for empty input, 404 if no Organization matches, and 403 when the caller lacks access.

get.summary: Get suggested graph queries for an entity
get.description: Returns a set of suggested graph search queries for the entity, used to power the "Related" / suggested searches surfaces in the UI. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs and 404 if the entity does not exist.

get.summary: Get suggested graph queries for an entity
get.description: Returns a set of suggested graph search queries for the entity, used to power the "Related" / suggested searches surfaces in the UI. Returns 400 for invalid GUIDs and 404 if the entity does not exist.

Returns the entities related to the supplied entity through a specific relationship, combining incoming and outgoing endpoints. Returns 404 when no entities match the relationship. Defaults: `page=0`, `take=10`.

Returns the entities related to the supplied entity through a specific relationship, combining incoming and outgoing endpoints. Returns 404 when no entities match the relationship. Defaults: `page=0`, `take=10`.

Returns users whose email domain matches the organisation's configured email domain, excluding the current caller and users that already have a CluedIn profile. Returns 204 No Content when the organisation has no email domain configured, and 404 if the caller's user entity cannot be found.

Returns users whose email domain matches the organisation's configured email domain, excluding the current caller and users that already have a CluedIn profile. Returns 204 No Content when the organisation has no email domain configured, and 404 if the caller's user entity cannot be found.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma/semicolon/pipe-separated list of source entity ids.

post.description: Creates outgoing edges of the supplied relationship type from each source entity to the target entity, and journals each addition. The `ids` parameter accepts comma, semicolon, or pipe-separated identifiers. Returns 406 when the relationship is not a known `EntityEdgeType`, 404 when the target entity does not exist, and 202 Accepted on success. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma/semicolon/pipe-separated list of source entity ids.

post.description: Creates outgoing edges of the supplied relationship type from each source entity to the target entity, and journals each addition. The `ids` parameter accepts comma, semicolon, or pipe-separated identifiers. Returns 406 when the relationship is not a known `EntityEdgeType`, 404 when the target entity does not exist, and 202 Accepted on success. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma/semicolon/pipe-separated list of source entity ids.

post.description: Removes outgoing edges of the supplied relationship type from each source entity to the target entity and journals each removal. Same id parsing and validation rules as `POST /entity/associate`. Returns 202 Accepted on success. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma/semicolon/pipe-separated list of source entity ids.

post.description: Removes outgoing edges of the supplied relationship type from each source entity to the target entity and journals each removal. Same id parsing and validation rules as `POST /entity/associate`. Returns 202 Accepted on success. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

get.description: Returns the serialised version history of an entity (a JSON document of the blob-store version history). **Deprecated** — use `GET /api/entityhistory/{id}` on the dedicated history controller instead.

Returns the serialised version history of an entity (a JSON document of the blob-store version history). **Deprecated** — use `GET /api/entityhistory/{id}` on the dedicated history controller instead.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Optional lower bound on change timestamps.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Optional upper bound on change timestamps.

get.description: Returns a summary of changes to an entity grouped by source, optionally filtered by date range. **Deprecated** — use `GET /api/entityhistory/list/{id}` instead.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Optional lower bound on change timestamps.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Optional upper bound on change timestamps.

get.description: Returns a summary of changes to an entity grouped by source, optionally filtered by date range. **Deprecated** — use `GET /api/entityhistory/list/{id}` instead.
Returns the per-property changes for a single data part in the entity's history. **Deprecated** — use `GET /api/entityhistory/details/{id}/{partId}` instead.
Returns the per-property changes for a single data part in the entity's history. **Deprecated** — use `GET /api/entityhistory/details/{id}/{partId}` instead.

parameters.name: includeShadowEntities
parameters.description: Include shadow entity types. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: includeExternalData
parameters.description: Include externally-sourced entity types. Defaults to true.

get.description: Alias for `GET /api/v1/entityTypes` exposed under the GDPR-themed `/api/gdpr/entityTypes` path. Same response shape and semantics.

parameters.name: includeShadowEntities
parameters.description: Include shadow entity types. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: includeExternalData
parameters.description: Include externally-sourced entity types. Defaults to true.

get.description: Returns the entity-type facets known to the organisation. Optional `entityType` filter narrows the result to a single type. Flags `includeShadowEntities` and `includeExternalData` control whether shadow and external-data entities contribute to the facet counts. (Also exposed as `GET /api/gdpr/entityTypes`.)

Returns the version-history branches for an entity, each with its name, the origin provider definitions that contributed to it, and the list of versions (data part id, input source, parent and child part ids). Used by version-history visualisations.

Returns counts and slim-info lists for the providers, sources, enrichers, clean projects, deduplication projects, AI agents, and export streams that the entity participates in. Returns 404 if the entity does not exist. Source ids and stream membership are read from the search store and stream log service for efficiency.

post.description: Merges a set of source entities into a target entity, dispatching a merge-with-user-input command and starting a workflow that records merge context (ids, user-supplied property choices, etc.). Source ids are comma, semicolon, or pipe-separated. Returns 400 when the target entity is missing and 202 Accepted when the merge command is queued. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Merges a set of source entities into a target entity, dispatching a merge-with-user-input command and starting a workflow that records merge context (ids, user-supplied property choices, etc.). Source ids are comma, semicolon, or pipe-separated. Returns 400 when the target entity is missing and 202 Accepted when the merge command is queued. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Optional source entity identifier; defaults to the caller's user entity.

get.description: Returns the all-shortest-paths between the caller's user entity (or a supplied `sourceId`) and the target entity. The Cypher query walks up to 5 hops and excludes temporal, code, and other technical relationships. Returns an empty list when neither endpoint can be found. Cached for 400 seconds.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Optional source entity identifier; defaults to the caller's user entity.

get.description: Returns the all-shortest-paths between the caller's user entity (or a supplied `sourceId`) and the target entity. The Cypher query walks up to 5 hops and excludes temporal, code, and other technical relationships. Returns an empty list when neither endpoint can be found. Cached for 400 seconds.

Returns the shortest graph paths from the caller's user entity to the target entity that go through Person/User/Contact/Organization nodes. Excludes temporal, code, follow, and similar technical relationships. Useful for "who knows whom" surfaces. Cached for 400 seconds.

Returns the shortest graph paths from the caller's user entity to the target entity that go through Person/User/Contact/Organization nodes. Excludes temporal, code, follow, and similar technical relationships. Useful for "who knows whom" surfaces. Cached for 400 seconds.
Returns the property names available for an entity-property filter, scoped by the supplied `PropertyScope` (defaults to `All`). Used by the UI to render property pickers for filters.
Returns the property names available for an entity-property filter, scoped by the supplied `PropertyScope` (defaults to `All`). Used by the UI to render property pickers for filters.

post.description: Validates and starts a background job that runs the supplied rule actions against entities matching the filter condition. Returns 400 when the condition is missing, the rule actions are empty, any rule has no actions, an action is not allowed in the current context, or an action is broken/misconfigured. Returns 200 with the distributed job id when the job has been started. (The path uses the legacy typo `enittyActions` — `/api/entityActions/runActions` is the alternate non-v1 path.)

get.description: Returns the rule actions available to the entity-actions runner for the current organisation. Used by the UI to populate the action picker when composing batched actions.

post.summary: Delete entity data by provider definition

parameters.name: considerManualUserInputInsignificant
parameters.description: When true, manual user input on affected entities is treated as insignificant and does not block deletion.

post.description: Starts a distributed job that deletes all entity data ingested through the given provider definition. Returns the job id on success and 400 if the provider definition id is empty or the criteria is invalid. The action is recorded in the audit log. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.summary: Delete entity data by provider definition

parameters.name: considerManualUserInputInsignificant
parameters.description: When true, manual user input on affected entities is treated as insignificant and does not block deletion.

post.description: Starts a distributed job that deletes all entity data ingested through the given provider definition. Returns the job id on success and 400 if the provider definition id is empty or the criteria is invalid. The action is recorded in the audit log. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.summary: Delete entity data by ingestion source

parameters.name: considerManualUserInputInsignificant
parameters.description: When true, manual user input on affected entities is treated as insignificant and does not block deletion.

post.description: Starts a distributed job that deletes entity data ingested through a specific ingestion source on a provider definition. Returns the job id on success and 400 if either id is empty or the criteria is invalid. Audit-logged. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.summary: Delete entity data by ingestion source

parameters.name: considerManualUserInputInsignificant
parameters.description: When true, manual user input on affected entities is treated as insignificant and does not block deletion.

post.description: Starts a distributed job that deletes entity data ingested through a specific ingestion source on a provider definition. Returns the job id on success and 400 if either id is empty or the criteria is invalid. Audit-logged. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.summary: Delete entity data by ingestion correlation id

parameters.name: considerManualUserInputInsignificant
parameters.description: When true, manual user input on affected entities is treated as insignificant and does not block deletion.

post.description: Starts a distributed job that deletes entity data ingested with a specific correlation id (typically a single ingestion run) on a provider definition, optionally narrowed by ingestion source. Returns 400 when required ids are empty. Audit-logged. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.summary: Delete entity data by ingestion correlation id

parameters.name: considerManualUserInputInsignificant
parameters.description: When true, manual user input on affected entities is treated as insignificant and does not block deletion.

post.description: Starts a distributed job that deletes entity data ingested with a specific correlation id (typically a single ingestion run) on a provider definition, optionally narrowed by ingestion source. Returns 400 when required ids are empty. Audit-logged. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Attempts to cancel an in-progress data deletion job for the given provider definition. Returns `{ Sucessful: true|false }` depending on whether a running job was found and cancellation was issued. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Attempts to cancel an in-progress data deletion job for the given provider definition. Returns `{ Sucessful: true|false }` depending on whether a running job was found and cancellation was issued. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.
Attempts to cancel an in-progress data deletion job for the given ingestion source on a provider definition. Returns `{ Sucessful: true|false }`. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.
Attempts to cancel an in-progress data deletion job for the given ingestion source on a provider definition. Returns `{ Sucessful: true|false }`. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Attempts to cancel an in-progress data deletion job for the given ingestion correlation id, optionally narrowed by ingestion source. Returns `{ Sucessful: true|false }`. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Attempts to cancel an in-progress data deletion job for the given ingestion correlation id, optionally narrowed by ingestion source. Returns `{ Sucessful: true|false }`. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

get.description: Returns the current progress and status of the deletion job for the given provider definition.
Returns the current progress and status of the deletion job for the given provider definition.
Returns the current progress and status of the deletion job for the given ingestion source on a provider definition.
Returns the current progress and status of the deletion job for the given ingestion source on a provider definition.
Returns the current progress and status of the deletion job for the given ingestion correlation id, optionally narrowed by ingestion source.
Returns the current progress and status of the deletion job for the given ingestion correlation id, optionally narrowed by ingestion source.

get.summary: Get deletion progress for multiple providers
get.description: Returns the current progress and status of deletion jobs for many provider definitions in one call, keyed by provider definition id. Returns 400 if the supplied criteria is invalid.

get.summary: Get deletion progress for multiple providers
get.description: Returns the current progress and status of deletion jobs for many provider definitions in one call, keyed by provider definition id. Returns 400 if the supplied criteria is invalid.

delete.description: Deletes a single entity by id by dispatching a delete-by-user-action command. Caller must be an administrator, hold Administration.Data RACI at Consultant level or above, or hold Integrations.ManualDataEntryInput at Consultant level or above. Returns 403 when the caller lacks the required claims, 404 when the entity does not exist, and 400 when the entity cannot be deleted (organisation root entity, the caller themselves, an organisation user entity, or partial access). Returns 202 Accepted when the delete command has been queued.

Deletes a single entity by id by dispatching a delete-by-user-action command. Caller must be an administrator, hold Administration.Data RACI at Consultant level or above, or hold Integrations.ManualDataEntryInput at Consultant level or above. Returns 403 when the caller lacks the required claims, 404 when the entity does not exist, and 400 when the entity cannot be deleted (organisation root entity, the caller themselves, an organisation user entity, or partial access). Returns 202 Accepted when the delete command has been queued.

Deletes a list of entities, dispatching one delete command per entity. Same claim requirements and per-entity safety checks as the single-entity variant (cannot delete the organisation root, the caller themselves, or organisation user entities). Returns 403 / 404 / 400 with details if any item fails validation; on success returns 202 Accepted after all commands have been queued.

Deletes a list of entities, dispatching one delete command per entity. Same claim requirements and per-entity safety checks as the single-entity variant (cannot delete the organisation root, the caller themselves, or organisation user entities). Returns 403 / 404 / 400 with details if any item fails validation; on success returns 202 Accepted after all commands have been queued.

get.summary: Check whether a data-part deletion job is complete
get.description: Returns true when the supplied distributed job is not in progress or stopping (i.e. it has finished, failed, or been cancelled). Returns 404 if the job id cannot be resolved.

get.summary: Check whether a data-part deletion job is complete
get.description: Returns true when the supplied distributed job is not in progress or stopping (i.e. it has finished, failed, or been cancelled). Returns 404 if the job id cannot be resolved.

get.summary: Check whether an entity can be deleted
get.description: Returns whether the caller can delete the supplied entity along with the reason if they cannot. Reasons include the entity being the organisation root, the caller themselves, an organisation user record, missing RACI claims, or insufficient access-control coverage. Returns 404 if the entity does not exist.

get.summary: Check whether an entity can be deleted
get.description: Returns whether the caller can delete the supplied entity along with the reason if they cannot. Reasons include the entity being the organisation root, the caller themselves, an organisation user record, missing RACI claims, or insufficient access-control coverage. Returns 404 if the entity does not exist.

post.summary: Check whether entities can be deleted
post.description: Returns the per-entity can-delete decision for a list of entity ids. Each entry follows the same rules as the single-entity check. Returns 404 with the offending id if any entity does not exist.

post.summary: Check whether entities can be deleted
post.description: Returns the per-entity can-delete decision for a list of entity ids. Each entry follows the same rules as the single-entity check. Returns 404 with the offending id if any entity does not exist.

delete.summary: Delete a single entity data part
delete.description: Deletes one data part from an entity. Returns 404 if the entity or the data part cannot be found, and 400 if the data part cannot be deleted (for example because it is the entity's only contributing data). Returns 202 Accepted with a job handle when the action has been queued. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

delete.summary: Delete a single entity data part
delete.description: Deletes one data part from an entity. Returns 404 if the entity or the data part cannot be found, and 400 if the data part cannot be deleted (for example because it is the entity's only contributing data). Returns 202 Accepted with a job handle when the action has been queued. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Deletes multiple data parts on a single entity in one call. Returns 404 if the entity or any data part cannot be found and 400 listing the ids of data parts that cannot be deleted. Returns 202 Accepted with a job handle on success. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Deletes multiple data parts on a single entity in one call. Returns 404 if the entity or any data part cannot be found and 400 listing the ids of data parts that cannot be deleted. Returns 202 Accepted with a job handle on success. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

get.summary: Check whether a data part can be deleted
get.description: Returns whether a specific data part can be removed from an entity and the reason if it cannot. Returns 404 if the entity or data part does not exist. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

get.summary: Check whether a data part can be deleted
get.description: Returns whether a specific data part can be removed from an entity and the reason if it cannot. Returns 404 if the entity or data part does not exist. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

post.summary: Check whether data parts can be deleted (bulk)
post.description: Returns the per-data-part can-delete decision for a list of data part ids on an entity. If the body is empty, every data part on the entity is checked. Returns 404 if the entity or any data part cannot be found. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim at View level or above.

post.summary: Check whether data parts can be deleted (bulk)
post.description: Returns the per-data-part can-delete decision for a list of data part ids on an entity. If the body is empty, every data part on the entity is checked. Returns 404 if the entity or any data part cannot be found. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim at View level or above.

get.description: Returns the serialised change history for the entity with the supplied id. Returns 400 if the id is not a valid GUID, 404 if the entity does not exist, and 403 if the caller lacks access.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Optional lower bound on change timestamps.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Optional upper bound on change timestamps.

get.description: Returns a summary of the entity's change history filtered by source, date range, and sort. **Deprecated** — prefer `GET /api/entityhistory/list/{id}`.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Optional list of vocabulary keys to include.

parameters.name: includeDetail
parameters.description: When true, include the full per-entry detail.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Optional lower bound on change timestamps.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: Optional upper bound on change timestamps.

get.description: Returns the filtered list of entity history entries. Supports filtering by source, origin, vocabulary keys, and date range, and can include the full detail per entry.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Optional list of vocabulary keys to include.

get.description: Returns the full detail of a single change (data part) in the entity's history, optionally restricted to a list of vocabulary keys.

get.summary: Get changed keys for entity history
get.description: Returns the vocabulary keys that have changed across the entity's history. Useful as a quick summary before drilling into individual change details.
Returns a compact view of the entity types available to the organisation, suitable for selectors and other UI controls that do not need the full entity type record.
Returns a compact view of the entity types available to the organisation, suitable for selectors and other UI controls that do not need the full entity type record.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Exact entity type to filter by.

parameters.name: onlyFromData
parameters.description: When true, return only entity types that have data. Defaults to false.

get.description: Returns the active entity types in the organisation, merged with the entity types discovered in actual data. Supports filtering by exact type, free-text search over display name, and a flag to only return entity types that have data.

put.description: Updates an existing entity type record (active flag, display name, icon, color, layout configuration, route, type, organisation id). If the supplied model id is `Guid.Empty`, the call is internally redirected to `POST /api/v1/entityinfo`. Returns 404 if the entity type does not exist.

post.description: Creates a new entity type record with the supplied active flag, display name, icon, color, layout configuration, route, and type. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for entity-type creation and publishes a remote `AddEntityType` event.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Exact entity type to filter by.

parameters.name: onlyFromData
parameters.description: When true, return only entity types that have data. Defaults to false.

get.description: Returns the active entity types in the organisation, merged with the entity types discovered in actual data. Supports filtering by exact type, free-text search over display name, and a flag to only return entity types that have data.

put.description: Updates an existing entity type record (active flag, display name, icon, color, layout configuration, route, type, organisation id). If the supplied model id is `Guid.Empty`, the call is internally redirected to `POST /api/v1/entityinfo`. Returns 404 if the entity type does not exist.

post.description: Creates a new entity type record with the supplied active flag, display name, icon, color, layout configuration, route, and type. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for entity-type creation and publishes a remote `AddEntityType` event.

get.description: Returns a paged list of entity types matching the search filter, in the legacy paged shape `{ data, total }`.
Returns a paged list of entity types matching the search filter, in the legacy paged shape `{ data, total }`.

post.description: Creates, modifies, and deletes outgoing entity edges from a single payload, dispatching the modifications as one background job. Returns 400 when all three edge collections (create, modify, delete) are empty. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim. Returns the distributed job handle on success.

Creates, modifies, and deletes outgoing entity edges from a single payload, dispatching the modifications as one background job. Returns 400 when all three edge collections (create, modify, delete) are empty. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim. Returns the distributed job handle on success.

Uploads a new preview image (binary body) for an entity. Returns 400 when the request body exceeds the configured upload limit (default in MB) or is not a valid image, 404 if the entity does not exist. If the change is intercepted by an approval workflow, returns 202 Accepted with the `X-CluedIn-SentForPowerAutomateApproval: true` header; otherwise returns 200 with the executing job handle. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Uploads a new preview image (binary body) for an entity. Returns 400 when the request body exceeds the configured upload limit (default in MB) or is not a valid image, 404 if the entity does not exist. If the change is intercepted by an approval workflow, returns 202 Accepted with the `X-CluedIn-SentForPowerAutomateApproval: true` header; otherwise returns 200 with the executing job handle. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

Applies a property modification (set/clear properties, add/remove aliases, tags, external references, set name/description/displayName/type/URI) to a single entity. Returns 400 when the entity id is empty, the model fails to bind, or the URI is malformed; 403 when the caller lacks the metadata-level Administration.Data claim required for metadata fields (name, description, aliases, tags, etc.); 404 if the entity does not exist. When the modification is intercepted by the approval pipeline, returns 202 Accepted with `X-CluedIn-SentForPowerAutomateApproval: true`; otherwise returns 200 with the executing job handle.

Applies a property modification (set/clear properties, add/remove aliases, tags, external references, set name/description/displayName/type/URI) to a single entity. Returns 400 when the entity id is empty, the model fails to bind, or the URI is malformed; 403 when the caller lacks the metadata-level Administration.Data claim required for metadata fields (name, description, aliases, tags, etc.); 404 if the entity does not exist. When the modification is intercepted by the approval pipeline, returns 202 Accepted with `X-CluedIn-SentForPowerAutomateApproval: true`; otherwise returns 200 with the executing job handle.

Applies property modifications to many entities in a single request, dispatching one batch of commands. Same validation rules as the single-entity variant: 400 for invalid URIs, 403 when the caller lacks the metadata-level Administration.Data claim for metadata fields, 404 when any referenced entity is missing. When any command is intercepted by the approval pipeline, the whole batch returns 202 Accepted with `X-CluedIn-SentForPowerAutomateApproval: true`.

Applies property modifications to many entities in a single request, dispatching one batch of commands. Same validation rules as the single-entity variant: 400 for invalid URIs, 403 when the caller lacks the metadata-level Administration.Data claim for metadata fields, 404 when any referenced entity is missing. When any command is intercepted by the approval pipeline, the whole batch returns 202 Accepted with `X-CluedIn-SentForPowerAutomateApproval: true`.

get.description: Returns the origins (the original source records) that contributed to the entity with the supplied id. Returns 400 if the id is not a valid GUID, 404 if the entity cannot be found, and 403 if the caller lacks access.

Returns the source providers that contributed data to the entity with the supplied id. Returns 400 if the id is not a valid GUID, 404 if the entity cannot be found, and 403 if the caller lacks access to the entity's providers.

parameters.name: filterConnectingNodesWithOnlyOneConnection
parameters.description: Drop intermediate connecting nodes that have only one connection. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: resolveFuzzyMatchRelationships
parameters.description: Include fuzzy-match relationships resolved by the topology service. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: filterOutMissingDataPartNodes
parameters.description: Hide graph nodes that correspond to missing data parts. Defaults to true.

get.description: Returns the relationship-topology graph for an entity. Use the query flags to filter the graph — for example, dropping connecting nodes that have only one connection, resolving fuzzy-match relationships, or hiding nodes for missing data parts. Returns 404 if the entity cannot be found.

parameters.name: filterIsUsed
parameters.description: When set, restrict to entity types that are (or are not) in use.

parameters.name: includePageTemplate
parameters.description: When true, include the entity type's page template in the result. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: includeShadowEntities
parameters.description: When true, include shadow entity types. Defaults to false.

get.description: Returns information for a single entity type, optionally filtered by free-text search, the type's usage state, whether to include the page template, and whether to include shadow entity types. Returns 404 if no matching entity type exists.

put.description: Updates an existing entity type from the supplied model. When the model id is `Guid.Empty` the call is internally redirected to `POST /api/entitytypeinfo` (Elasticsearch-sourced models may not always include a GUID; see internal notes in the controller). Returns 404 if the entity type cannot be found.

post.description: Creates a new entity type from the supplied model. Returns 409 Conflict when an entity type with the same technical name already exists. The action goes through the RACI approval pipeline for entity-type creation and publishes a remote `AddEntityType` event.

parameters.name: filterIsUsed
parameters.description: When set, restrict to entity types that are (or are not) in use.

parameters.name: includePageTemplate
parameters.description: When true, include the entity type's page template. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: includeShadowEntities
parameters.description: When true, include shadow entity types. Defaults to false.

get.description: Returns a paged list of entity types matching the supplied search and source filters. Used by the UI selectors to browse available entity types.

get.summary: Check whether an entity type exists

parameters.name: entityType
parameters.description: Entity type technical name to check.

get.description: Returns whether an entity type with the supplied technical name exists. Returns 400 when `entityType` is empty.
Returns every identity counter configured for the organisation along with its name, current minimum id, and current maximum id.

post.description: Creates a new identity counter with the supplied name and optional min/max id range. Returns 400 when the name is missing or when `minId > maxId`, and 409 Conflict if an identity counter with the same name already exists.

put.description: Updates the min/max id of an existing identity counter. Returns 400 when the name is missing or when `minId > maxId`, and 404 when no identity counter with that name exists.

get.description: Returns topology-graph information for a specific data part on an entity. Used by the split-entity UI to render the source data attached to a node before split.
Returns graph node information for an entity code on an entity. The code segment is URL-decoded before lookup. Returns 400 if the code is malformed.
Returns graph node information for a merge that originated from a deduplication project for the given entity and merge data part.
Returns graph node information for a manual merge on the given entity and merge data part.

post.description: Returns a preview of the split that would result from applying the supplied split action to an entity, without performing the split. Returns 400 if the action descriptor is malformed and 404 if the entity does not exist.

Starts a distributed job that splits an entity according to the supplied action. Returns the job handle so callers can poll progress. Returns 400 for invalid actions and 404 if the entity does not exist.
